items.title: k-Means
items.description: k-means partitions data into k spherical groups around centroids and assigns every point. DBSCAN discovers density-connected regions without fixing k and explicitly labels noise. Use k-means for similar-sized blobs; DBSCAN for irregular shapes and outliers.

items.title: Distance Metrics
items.description: DBSCAN's eps-neighborhoods are defined by a distance function (typically Euclidean). Metric choice changes which points are neighbors — see Distance Metrics for comparing metrics; this topic assumes a metric is chosen and focuses on density-based cluster formation.

items.title: Cluster Evaluation
items.description: After fitting DBSCAN, assess stability, silhouette on non-noise points, and domain plausibility. Cluster Evaluation is the canonical home for metrics and validation workflows; here you learn what eps and min_samples produce structurally.

items.title: Unsupervised Learning
items.description: DBSCAN is a clustering method under unsupervised learning: it discovers group structure from unlabeled X without predicting y. The unsupervised paradigm — hypotheses to validate, not ground-truth labels — is defined in Unsupervised Learning.
